mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card behavior on Print and Payment History page when a plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages, additional pages, progress bars, tooltips, and page count displays.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires printer enrollment functionality. This batch contains cancellation and enrollment confirmation page objects. While ConfirmationPage has extensive enrollment flow locators (plan selection, shipping, billing), these are for the UI-based enrollment process during initial subscription setup, not for programmatic printer enrollment needed as a test precondition. The test requires an API helper or setup method to enroll a printer with a specific plan before the test begins. No such API helper or enrollment setup method exists in this batch. The enrollment page objects in this batch are for user-facing enrollment flows, not test data setup.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires verification of subscription state (no free months). This batch contains cancellation flow page objects with subscription-related elements, but none provide methods to check or verify subscription details like free months status. No API helper, getter method, or verification method found in this batch that can retrieve or validate subscription free months information.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires verification that subscription status is 'subscribed'. This batch contains cancellation page objects that deal with subscription state changes (cancelling, pausing, resuming), but no methods to verify the current subscription status. No API helper or verification method found that can check subscription status.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires pausing the plan before the main test steps. CancellationPage in this batch contains a pause_plan_link locator ([data-testid='pause-your-plan'], [data-testid='cancellation-alternative-pause-your-plan']) and a pause_plan_modal locator. However, these are retention alternatives shown DURING the cancellation flow to prevent users from cancelling. The test case requires pausing a plan as a setup step from the dashboard or main account page, not as a cancellation retention alternative. The context is completely different - the test needs to pause an active subscription from the normal dashboard flow, while this batch's pause elements are part of the cancellation intervention/retention strategy. Additionally, there are no action methods in this batch to actually click the pause link or handle the pause modal - only locators are defined. The actual pause plan functionality needed for this test precondition likely exists in a different batch containing dashboard or account management page objects.

test_case_mappings.no_match_reason: This test case (C44873414) tests the Billing Cycle Period card behavior on the Print and Payment History page during a paused plan scenario, including verification of complimentary pages progress bars, additional pages progress bars, tooltips, page count displays, and visual elements like progress bar colors. The test requires: (1) PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage page object with locators for billing_cycle_period_card, plan_pause_information, complimentary_pages_progress_bar, complimentary_pages_value_text, complimentary_pages_info_icon, complimentary_pages_tooltip, additional_pages_progress_bar, additional_pages_value_text, additional_pages_info_icon, additional_pages_tooltip, total_pages_printed_text, and various info messages. (2) DashboardPage for navigation to Print and Payment History. (3) API helpers for printer enrollment, subscription management, time shifting (32 days), billing charge triggering, and page printing simulation. (4) Verification methods for progress bar colors (black, yellow), progress bar fill percentages, tooltip display and content, text value matching, and element visibility. (5) Utility methods for device type detection (desktop vs mobile/tablet), responsive interaction handling (hover vs click), progress bar color extraction, and CSS property validation. This batch (batch 11) contains five page objects focused entirely on cancellation and enrollment flows: CancellationBannerPage (cancellation banner component with resume subscription elements), CancellationPage (comprehensive cancellation flow with 78 locators for cancellation summary, feedback survey, change plan modal, retention alternatives), CancellationTimelinePage (cancellation timeline modal with 31 locators for timeline steps and restoration), ChangePlanCancellationPage (change plan modal with 30 locators for plan selection and billing cycle options), and ConfirmationPage (enrollment confirmation flow with 217 locators for plan selection, shipping, billing, payment methods, and enrollment finalization). None of these page objects relate to the Print and Payment History page, billing cycle period cards, page usage tracking, complimentary/additional pages display, or progress bar visualization. The functional domains are completely separate: this batch covers subscription cancellation and initial enrollment, while the test case covers ongoing subscription management, page usage monitoring, and billing cycle display during a paused state. The Print and Payment History page object, dashboard navigation, API helpers for time manipulation and page printing, and progress bar verification utilities needed for this test case are not present in this batch and likely exist in different batches containing account management, dashboard, billing, or utility modules.
